channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 241 replies — 255.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 241 • Updated: 2026-07-10 09:43 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464714
>>62464342
RICO ROUNDUPPPPPOOO

62464716
>>62464052
>>62464263
I want to get paid for my investment and don't care about any of these people. But it would be a bonus if PP got prosecuted for financial crime.

62464724
>>62464716
There won't be any political capital to go after someone like PP when the short hedgie crimes are piling up and causing some very serious issues for the U.S. capital markets. They'll want to pay us and sweep this under the rug. Prosecuting PP would not go over well when his audience, now millionaires, start making a stink.

62464731
>>62464724
I have a hard time believing anybody gives a shit about that guy

62464732
>>62437938

62464772
>>62464731
When we find out that we won the PP show is going to be the place to be. People may be sick of the grifting and the baseless and endless hype posts he makes but once it finally happens even the haters will be watching.

62464881
>>62441565
>Amelie Warren
that's some terrible taste lmao

62464891
>>62464772
The haters are already watching Kek. They can’t stop talking about his wife in both threads and they’re literally reading court filings for his divorce.

62464895
>>62464891
They are convinced that Sonichu is both PP and ABC in disguise.

62464897
>>62464895
PP HAS to be every towel knower.

62464909
>>62464895
>everyone is PP
I am not PP, debunked. Shills can not dispute the holiday inn dilemma

62464919
>>62464772
$GABBBYQ has already won, this is why she hasn’t been since the 18th

62464943
I’m starting to think this is a larp

62464998
>>62464943
>The CUSIP Conundrum, the Director Dilemma, and the Affable Affiliates are all an elaborate ruse.

62465011
>>62464998
>The Gabbbyq Gape at the Holiday Inn
Interesting

62465133
>>62464943
What does live action role playing have to do with the bankruptcy of bed bath and beyond?

62465151
>>62464047
that's the thing about starting a religion. You have to figure out how to monetize it

so he creates a proxy for BBBY and then pretends it's being crucified by the romans just like your messiah was. Then he can trick some of you fools into buying it before rugging you like the dumbass sheep you are.

>>62464998
those things are obvious schizophrenic apophenia. What's not clear is if your schizophrenia is real or affected.

62465330
The shills were instructed to spam shit about the personal life of theppshow host because pulte(director of national intelligence) frequenting it and Larry cheng going on after shares were cancelled, were huge signals that told retail bbbyq is coming back.

Even more important is that the cusip-6 of bbbyq and the bbby warrants match. Shillies stopped having their mods frantically delete posts last fall/winter and now they just spam and flood to try and avoid the curiosity altogether.

62465397
>>62465330
Ok debunk the divorce filings in Utah and why Mrs PP has not been seen since the 18th and Ryan Cohen tweeted a T Rex image in the Bahamas

You can’t shilly willy

62465412
all they have left is forced satire and inauthentic contrived meme energy that never sticks which is why the AMC spam (attempt to discredit the CUSIP-6) stopped because this stuff never works and people see right through it.

62465418
>>62465412
silo the topic as much as you want, you can’t refute the holiday inn

62465422
>>62465418
I guess I'll take the bait.

You think focusing on the shows host is more important that the content discussed on the show? Or prominent figures who went onto the show who are very close to RC? Of course you don't because you're paid to be here.

62465431
>>62465422
I fully agree with you, Ryan has been inside the PP suite at the holiday inn as well, we all have. We are all waiting 9 months now for the divorce to conclude and we can all celebrate, at the holiday inn.

62465456
You'd think the shill sissies would be uploading these divorce documents or whatever the fuck they're talking about since they already uploaded PP's resume and everything else. Makes you wonder if they're just...making things up with zero basis whatsoever. Hard to blame them cause these boomers and jeets really can't meme. Between dougshit, CUSIP Conundrum, etc. they really have no way of controlling sentiment so they just make the lamest copies trying to capture any bit of meme energy.

62465498
>>62465456
Do your own research, the cuck chair rarely comes pre heated, but when it does, PP is there.

Funny how you haven’t disproven the (1) thing yet you refuse discuss, interesting

62465544
>>62465456

62465594
>>62465544
>no mention of his devoted wife
Divorce is ongoing, nothing happens until it concludes.

GME bulls have won

62465636
>>62465151
>your messiah
Kek

62466545
CUSIP-6

62466579
>>62466545
>ignores the real conundrum
GME Bulls are in control

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 74 replies — 210.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-07-10 10:19 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

Early replies (first 3)

62453729
All you faggots need to do is buy chainlink. Fuck your side hustles. You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.

62453795
Last thread we spoke a lot on learning new skills but very few fruitful things arose. I realized the issue is that a lot of the proposed skills were just boring or too niche.

62453799
>>62453795
wait, are you asking poor people to discuss amongst themselves how to get rich?
kek

you don't learn how to get rich from poor people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62461982
>>62457877
>>62457792
What sells a good memecoin is not only the narrative but the art accompanied alongside it. In this case, we know that

>Animals

Are positively received.

I cannot think of any legendary memecoin runner(multi-billion) that had a human at its front and center. It's usually animals, and commonly known animals like pets or frogs.

I think for this thread as we do our research on art we can also practice drawing as a way to become more valuable and ultimately increase our net worth.

62462009
Who here could obtain this job? All of you could, it will just come down to whether the individual hiring believes you have the qualifications.

Let's take a further examination into a high paying role such as this. Let's see what sort of requirements are needed to make $50 an hour.

62462035
you ever had a job that scaled like this?

62462047
Is this beyond your capabilities?

62462063
Is all your money in dollars?

Are you not aware that art itself can also be a place to store value in?

With a resurgence in European culture spreading out through the world, with the increase of non white races, won't people desire a form of art that was once dominant in the world?

ESPECIALLY as we get more and more digital?

62462167
>>62462035

>Question
>what makes art sell, what similarities did the memecoins ( >>62457792 ) &( >>62457877 )

Answer:
they were dogs

>Question
>what do we have to learn, make a high amount of money in a short amount of time?

Answer is right here --> >>62462047

Question
>What forms of art can we store money in

????

62462792
Have to go to bed,

Goodnight and bump

62462799
>>62457657
Must you broadcast your ethnic insecurity all over 4channel.org(slash) biz?

62462804
>>62454046
>mom look I posted it again!

62462813
I finally paid off my high Apr CC debt so now I can stack paychecks for a while. I’ve been good about cutting down spending in general. I wonder if I should cut out meat and just do rice and beans? Can you get similar nutrition from beans? Things are certainly not looking great because I care about my job less by the day but at least I paid off debt.

I don’t wanna /blog too hard but fuck life is sucking lately, I just hope it’s better in a year. I been working nonstop for two years haven’t had any pto either

62462824
>>62453622
>yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen
go work literally any job for like 6 months

62462992
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

OH NO NO NO

AHAHAHAHAHAHA

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

62464145
>>62462813
Rent truly is the worst bloodsucker. What are you saving for? Just to have reserve funds? You were spending all your excess money on debt and not saving/investing? Genuinely asking

62464163
>>62462813
Also I would be careful with poor nutrition depending on your age. If under 25 then take the risk with cheap foods.

62465232
Bump, almost done waging

62465510
alright today, lets practice looking at good designs of top runners and seeing if we can emulate the artists ability. Let's also look at great /biz/ memes and ask ourselves, ""what made this meme so popular" like it was an actual gallery

62465526
Who is this? What makes him so special? Why is he one of the most identifiable mascots on this site?

62465529
Forgot the image, what about this characters DESIGN made him so well loved

62465547
this is what knowyourmeme has to say on Mumu

side note: alt girls get paid to make videos like this? what a job!

62465548
what will happen when we reach #10k thread?

62465553
the absolute state of this board

62465556
>>62453622
Quads and I’ll chop my cock off

62465561
>>62465556
dude...

62465736
How did this character become the most recognizable image on /biz/?

62465765
and here is information on bobo

62465874
I realize I have lost some anons attentions, let us have a group activity. Let's all draw a variation of Bobo or Mumu. I will go ahead and start!

62465886
>>62465874
make sure you use a Bobo or Mumu as reference!

62466308
>>62465547
>alt girls get paid to make videos like this?
she's been making videos about memes for around 2 years, I guess she just got a deal with KYM now. I want to have sex with her btw

62466618
Fell asleep, will continue this tomorrow

item.title: Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late. — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-07-10 10:34 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466546
>>62466527
All China can do is copy others. The only Asian country that can innovate is Japan which is why they're honorary Aryans and chinks are bug people.

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

62466556
>>62466549
Singing toilets

62466590
>>62466548
Good as any other media

62466594
>>62466590
Is there any good quality video yet? every single one i saw looked like ai

62466599
>>62466594
What is real anyway? Is Iran war real?
How could plebs tell?

62466600
I'm going to buy spcx at $10

62466602
>>62466535
Or maybe the billionaires should start paying their engineers enough so that they actually feel like staying loyal?

62466603
>>62466527
I haven't heard of China selling launch services to foreign countries, either businesses or governments. Maybe they'll try, but who will trust them?

It's not just a question of government espionage, or corporate espionage, there's also the fact that China half-asses everything -- they call it "chàbuduō". Drop a satellite during transport? Don't tell the customer, they might blame you for the optics being all out of alignment or for the solar panels not opening properly!

The one really great thing about this is that maybe now China will stop crashing boosters onto their own citizens' villages. That's literally their current launch system -- figure out roughly where the booster is going to crash, tell those villages to evacuate, launch the rocket, watch it crash, and pick up the pieces. That happens even in successful launches, not just for failures. See picrelated. You can find a lot more photos, videos, and news articles with just one simple search.

62466607
>>62466603
Sounds like a lot of cope.

62466610
>>62466549
You know that monitor you're reading this on? It was made possible by a Japanese engineer spending five years mastering the egg, er, I mean, LED manufacturing, so that he could invent the first usable blue-colored LED. The entire rest of the civilized world had been trying to create functional blue LEDs for literally over 50 years and failed. The only thing anyone else had been able to make was very low-yield very weak blue LEDs that were unusable for anything serious.

Here's a really good video on it. I watch it several times a year for inspiration.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AF8d72mA41M

62466612
>>62466600
I'm going to buy ALL the SPCX at $11 just to fuck you over.

>>62466607
Dilate more.

62466614
>>62466612
>I'm going to buy ALL the SPCX at $11 just to fuck you over.
Something tells me you won't. You'd need Warren buffets entire net worth liquid to do that.

62466624
>>62466614
No I wouldn't. If SPCX dropped 94.444% to $11, half its total market cap would only be about $72B. (Elon's never going to sell his half of the company.) All I need is for Bitcoin to do another 70,000X by then.

62466646
>>62466610
These guys make really good content. Thanks.

62466649
>>62466527
SpaceX
https://youtu.be/RYUr-5PYA7s
https://youtube.com/shorts/dm5QqoqkU9o

China
https://youtube.com/shorts/s_BiDawHGjw

62466651
>>62466602
If loyalty is the issue then we should be mass ejecting every mainland Chinese from the west. The people spilling out secrets to the chinks are chinks working in the west.

item.title: I got hired at McDonald’s today. What am I in for? Should I just KMS? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I got hired at McDonald’s today. What am I in for? Should I just KMS?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-10 10:31 UTC

OP

I got hired at McDonald’s today. What am I in for? Should I just KMS?

Early replies (first 3)

62462389
>>62462380
I've heard it can be relatively comfy

62462391
You can intentionally mess up an order of something you might like to eat for free later

62462393
Put your head down, take as many hours as you can, and buy as much bitcoin and gold as you can while minimizing expenses.

Or just take the easy way out I guess. Pussy ass nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62462424
can be comfy if you work in a white area. God saves you if you work in beaner or nigger neighborhood

62462442
>>62462391
this, ganesh chundi walla THIS. white morality ALL timmy cope. if it there, it can be the scammed one million percent.

62462445
I'm working fast food at the moment. Even though I'm overqualified, I suck it up and remind myself that it's temporary.

62462457
I didn’t go to med school for this!

62462458
>>62462380
Stand in the lettuce and then post it on social media

62462460
>>62462380
probably try to get on night shift and dont answer the speaker/wait for people to just drive away. give hobos free food to terrorize people so they dont come maybe

62462477
>>62462380
how did you make it past the personality test?

62462567
>>62462389
If I were younger, I'd do it. I doubt it was ever that bad, it just got memed into reality that working there was the lowest of the lowest until eventually they upped their pay higher than the jobs most of the people who'd make fun of them earned.
Also, jobs like this where you get a meal or two out of it go a long ways. Food is usually the biggest expense after rent for someone in this stage of life. Even if it's not great food, the fact that you filled up and aren't immediately dropping $20 on takeout after work will go a long ways.
Scout for a location that has some women working there though. Who you work with makes or breaks a crumby job.

62462571
>>62462445
IMO you're better off working somewhere overqualified enough that it's obvious you're overqualified than taking a job people would mistake for being a serious.

62463469
>>62462567
>>62462571
>for being a serious

Nice double post btw. How’s the weather in Mumbai?

62466640
>>62462380
why didn't you choose Burger King tho?

item.title: I've invested $40K in ICP since 2022 — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've invested $40K in ICP since 2022

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-10 10:26 UTC

OP

My stack of ICP is now worth about $10K. AMA

Early replies (first 3)

62463800
Why would i ask a retard a question? That was rhetorical btw, so dont say "you just did".

62463814
>>62463800
I admit I'm retarded. I'm sharing my experience so I can help other potential retards avoid doing what I did.

62463818
>>62463789
Are you going to buy more? How does it feel to torture yourself like that?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62463844
>>62463818
I'm done. It hurt to realize that Dom and his team are mega-scammers who have disdain for those who bought into their vision for the World Computer

62464693
>>62463844
he's being (((suppressed)))

62464870
>>62463789
I've invested $55k with my current holdings being worth ~$17k.

62465954
I still haven’t seen a thread where someone claims that they gave up and sold their bags. The bottom is not in.

62465994
>My stack of ICP is now worth about $10K.
>AMA
okay
why

62466207
>>62463789
Why are you still holding? Didn't icy pee launch their magical AI site generator and it flopped hard?
What is the future prospect of ICP? Just rising tide from macros?

62466279
>>62463789
My losses are worse

62466283
>>62466207
They are coming out with a new Dex called “MultiDex” it’s suppose to be the ultimate dex that will connect every chain and coin, also caffeine AI continues to improve

62466292
>>62466279
>14K pee
what in the dom hell...
why??

62466303
>>62466292
I believed and DCA’d for a while. I could ape in now and get like 10x my stack but I am too demoralized on ICP to buy anymore. I figure this is a make it stack if it becomes a narrative next bull otherwise I don’t care if I lose rest of my money at this point

62466630
>>62463789
caffeine is an extremely shitty ripoff of replit which is already the most ripped off tech product of all time..

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-10 10:39 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62466465
>>62466461
Pretty sure these twatterfags are getting offers to shitmouth Link or hand over their handles, and that this paid fud campaign is ramping up especially now that it's all clearly coming to fruition.

62466645
>>62466449
Extremely bullish news for ChainlinkLabs
Extremely bearish news for baggies

$4 EOY

62466664
>>62466645
The more Chainlink is used, the more the nodes need to get paid in Link, since they literally can't be paid in anything but Link.

62466667
>>62466664
not true and you perfectly know this baggie

item.title: VOO and Chill — 206 replies — 60.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 206 • Updated: 2026-07-10 07:16 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62436562
>>62400493
>VOO
it's EVOO for me lads

62438775
Yep bros
Bought some more VOO and am waiting on my DRAM position to go green so I can sell and not have to deal with thematic ETFs no high expense ratios ever again
Simple as

62440169
>>62431374
>VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses
VOO is passively managed. These 2 girls are not like Kathy Wood picking stocks.

62442752
>>62431374
They could boil alive eat grannies alive for all I care as long as they get the money going, what kind of faggot ass train of thought is this. They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding. "The people making me rich are foids so it makes me icky" is unironically the foidest take I have ever read.
>>62430902
A year ago I started investing for the first time in my life (I'm in my mid 20s) and thought I was very smart and I would just outperform the market 1% profit at a time. After a whole year I only managed to get a net benefit of a 3% after wasting many hours and carrying heavy bags. I learnt my lesson. VOO and chill.

62442988
>>62442752
>They could become billionaires and I would not give a shit as long as it keeps compounding.

The portfolio managers of VOO have 0 control over whether VOO compounds or not. VOO is simply an ETF that tracks the S&P 500. Their only job is to make sure VOO follows the S&P 500. Once the fund is set up, there really is not much for them to do. I guess if they haven't automated removing and adding companies to the composition of VOO, then they do that.

62444521
Let’s all say thank you to Jack Bogle

62444531
>>62400515
and yet boomers hold 90% of the wealth and growing...

62445651
>>62421799
>5+ yr 60% drawdown event happens
>buy cheaper voo

62446571
>>62444521
thanks chad bogle

62447653
>>62445651
Kek it really is that simple

62450991
>>62446571
Thanks Chad Bogle!

62454214
>>62413718
Felix Prehn has never been an investment banker. He is an ex artist and wine salesman, now grifter on YouTube who makes money from his "academy" fees (up to 10k for shitty training). He's a regular scammer, like many in this segment.

62455460
It feels good to be a VOO’er

62455475
>>62400493
My retirement funds are $70k and in VOO, anything better to put all that in?

62455990
>>62455475
Look into VPMAX

62456048
>>62455990
Neat

62459187
>>62403053
The median 64 yo is planning to work until they're dead.
Boomers are never going to retire because they can't afford to. They are going to keep working like the good little goyim they are and fall down dead on the job because that's the only way they can fund the monthly payments on all the toys they went into debt to purchase.
I spit on their graves. Fuck boomer scum.

62459238
>>62459187
Lmao, another white incel who knows everything,,,,,what are the odds? 100% at least,,,,,do they use it to get rich? Lmao no

62460695
Yawwwwwn another week another VOO purchase

62460733
>>62460695
i made a whole $5 buying a share of VOO during that dip :) too bad I lost more from all my previous VOO shares

62460835
>>62460733
Sorry to hear that champ maybe DRAM is more your speed

62460844
>>62400995
Lmfao. You do realize that the highest weighted stocks that lead the spy are mostly in QQQ right? Lmfao

62460852
>>62459187
They can afford it

62461238
>>62401051
>I think a decade long CAGR comparison was a 5% difference but I’m not sure if CAGR is the best stat to go by?
It changes significantly depending on when you enter the market. Since 2000 there's only a 0.6% difference in favor of QQQ over SPY, the latter outperformed the former for most of that timeframe. If you bought sometime post-2009, however, you would have been better off holding QQQ by far. The moral of the story is keep an eye on things and only buy more volatile assets if it's a growth period. QQQ will continue to be great if you bought a decade ago until the music stops.

62461412
>>62400515
Fpbp. Bogleheads are the biggest retards on the planet.

62461672
>>62400620
>What makes one rich
Health, family, friends.

62462476
>>62461238
For now I’m still just doing VOO cause it just feels better even if I’m missing out on qqq gains

62464487
It’s payday tomorrow, VOO brahs. There’s nothing risky that looks too appealing so I go all voo maybe some palantir as well. My only regret is taking the work health benefits, my check will be smaller. Two weeks is an eternity to wait for your money.

62464830
Is there any point of voo and chill if I already have a 401k and pension. Know can compound it more but can be more risky right

62466388
>>62400493
buy lottery tickets

item.title: Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL… — 92 replies — 39.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL…

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-07-10 08:19 UTC

OP

Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
>Even if you're "beating inflation" with a mortgage you're paying much more than its actual cost
>Closing costs
>All just so you can sell it (likely at a loss when considering inflation) so you can buy a slightly less expensive house
It's not that buying a house is a bad purchase if you can afford it, but acting like it's somehow an "investment" is absolutely retarded, you'd probably make more back from buying Pokemon cards

Early replies (first 3)

62450381
>>62450377
You're supposed to buy houses on a discount
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL

62450391
>>62450377
Idk I just want one to live in, but maybe they rent it that way person who rents pays bills and when he break something you can tell him to pay for it and then when they don't give a fuck anymore they sell it

62450397
>cbf moving every year because the landlord decides they a wankers and want to sell to some Chinese immigrants who took our jobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62459229
>>62459207
marriage and children is the death of the individual.

62459231
>>62459229

And ?

62459234
>>62459229
Ew fuck off you selfish retard

62459240
>>62459229
Reddit moment. You gonna quote Nietzsche next?

62459243
>>62459234
>>62459240
shouldnt you be getting your wife's son ready for the day?

62459270
>>62459262
Based

62459719
>>62459227
>How much does that cost?
Typically 1-3% of the home value annually is the expectation on average but it seems more spaced out than that. I.e. roof goes bad 16k bill one year, next year maybe minimal expense basic maintenance items like filters, or an AC servicing for a few hundred.

>Optional if you own the home. Even then, I paid $1700 last year for insurance which is nothing. That’s easily 1 months rent by itself.
If you think home insurance is optional then lmao, I would never have a 300k+ possession that could be wiped out by some other idiots stupidity or natural disaster but you do you. Where I love its not too bad but certain states or areas have very high insurance costs (FL, CO, CA, etc.) Worth considering.

>Non issue
What if you get laid off? What if you get a job offer for 60% higher salary but it requires you to move 3 hours away?

>I can sell my house for more than I paid. You can’t sell the apartment that you dumped 5-6 figures into over the years. The choice is obvious
I can sell my shares of VOO too. It depends on where you live and if you inflation adjust property values. For example, where i live property has increased by 3.7% since 2023, that sounds pretty good until you realize that cumulative inflation was 9.84% over the same duration meaning you lost money relative to inflation in my region as a home owner. You also don't get to sell the interest the bank made off you during ownership, that piece of your mortgage payment (around 80%-85% for the first 5 years) is exactly like rent, poof. You also dont get back the real-estate transaction fees, realtor fee, and transfer taxes that is usually around 8-15k for a 300k house just gone. Homeowners also dump 5-6 figures into keeping their homes just in taxes usually. Let's be honest, owning a home is a nice thing but it costs you money and is not an asset, it is a necessity that you live somewhere but there are more efficient ways to use your capital if you want to be actually wealthy.

62460098
>>62459719
>$16k for a new roof
Who the fuck is paying $16k to have their roof reshingled? I paid $4500 years ago and I only did that because the old shingles were at least 20 years old and discolored. Other “maintenance and repair” expenses are minimal in the grand scheme of things.

>If you think home insurance is optional then lmao
It is… There is no law demanding a home owner, who owns a house that’s paid off (which mine is), to have insurance. The only reason I have it is because paying ~$1700ish a year is pocket change. I live in FL btw.

>what if…..
I’m not even going to bother. We can spend all day going back and forth playing this game.

62460105
>>62460098
I see anon prefers roofs with 5 year lifespans instead of 20.

62460136
>>62455984
I was going to say the same, got mine at 362k with 3.25% and I'm sitting on a gold mine. House already gained 150,000 in equity in 6 years.

62460152
>>62450377
It lets me fuck women behind door which is quite a good investment for my cock and balls and gives me the reasurement I'm not giving my money to some boomer faggot in exchange of nothing which is again a good investment for my soul.

62460218
Its a good investment if you rent it out and can make back what you spent on it, it becomes free-ish passive income, that said being a good landlord is a skill and shit ones cant break even, you are not some globohomo LLC who can take a total loss if you let in some nigger tenants

Its also a good investment if you are renting, because renting is literally throwing money into the trash, whore yourself our or sell crack but for the love of god find a way to get enough for a downpayment, every month of rent is a month of your life wasted

if you already have something or manipulated family members to stay with them rent free, real estate is a shit investment

62460250
>>62460098
Kekalek, do whatever you want, idc. Just because the math made sense in 2021 doesn't mean it does now. I just did the amortization and cost estimates with annual rent increases for 5 and 10 year costs, remove whatever you want to tilt the scales in your favor but right now new homeowners always lose. This math is for suburban midwest at current interest rates, my finding is new buyers get annihilated buying a house right now here so why buy one?

62460605
>>62460250
>Interest
It never began for you. Rent's due on the 1st.

62460668
>>62460605
Nice one, no arguments at all. Anyways enjoy paying for your "asset".
>Mortgage to your landlord, the bank, is due on the first
>Property tax due on July 15th and January 15th
>HOA fee due quarterly
>Remeber to pay the monthly CC bill and your personal loan your "4000 dollar roof".

62460697
>>62460668
>DEBATE ME
lol

62460704
>>62450377
It depends on the country. In my country interest rate on house loans is very low and the average rent is 50% or more of the average salary. The same monthly installment that gets you a pretty sokid house would be able to rent just a small studio, most of which are absolute dogshit. The difference is so extreme that the only people that don't get a mortgage in their mid 20s are either unemployed or planning on leaving the country alltogether.

62460713
>>62460098
between pulling the permits, Removing and disposing of the old roof, complexity of the job, and using standard Asphalt shingles, a 2000sq ft house is 15k easy. replaced my 3000sq ft house roof and it was 20k. this was after getting multiple quotes

62460729
>>62460098
also I'm in FL, the requirements are much more strict to meet insurance compliance then the rest of the US

62460731
I see a house as a home base from which you can plan all of your operations out of.

62460837
>>62459200
>moving into your aging parents paid off home to save even more while helping them in their daily lives while you "work" from home is le heckin saderino

Just my vanguard account makes more than you in any given day. Lol

62460839
>>62460105
most roofs outside of America last 80+ years lmao

62460861
>>62460837
what do you with that money if you're online all day and don't even have a woman? serious question. What will you do when you're 45 and want to retire but still need to rent?

62460869
>>62460839
Wrong

62462359
>>62450377
house is for if you have 2+ kids

62462473
>>62460861

62466410
>>62450377
better being a home owner than being a WEF cattle rentoid

62466418
>>62450377
>Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment

Maybe on planet retard

62466423
Cheapest form of leverage with tax write offs.

62466477
>>62450377
Who gives a fuck what fuckwits think. I get 600/week. Every week. For doing nothing. Plus I have no mortgages. Plus my investments are doubling every 10 years. Plus I sleep like a baby.

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 137 replies — 36.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 137 • Updated: 2026-07-10 06:57 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62458608
>>62458144
There is plenty you could do but you value your comfort over your country

62458622
>>62449846
LMAO

62458858
The jugaad is crazy

62460566
Jugaadjeet

62460624
>>62449441
Bloody bitch bastard

62460700
>>62449388
this is old news
I do this.
>18 months later mattress topper starts to hurt my back
>Whats the reason for the return?
>it hurts my back
>ok thank you sir, here is full refund

or the guy who returned his inflatable pool after a few months. He got the pool for his pet penguin that he ordered. after a few months and no penguin delivery he realized it was a scam and returned the pool and other things he got for the penguin.
>reason for return
>penguin order was a scam, no penguin arrived

I also know some dudes who buy wavestorms, go fuk em up, and exchange them

Costco puts the onus of returns on the manufacturer or supplier as a price for getting on the shelf in the store. just having a spot in costco is worth it enough to most companies to front all the returns for costco's lax return policy. Which is why costco DGAF and rather have customers.

62460720
>>62458144
serious question
I went bowling last week. There was a huge group of brown men. Enjoying themselves, very much, maybe too much.
They can not keep their hands off each other it was the strangest thing I've every seen.
Rubbing, patting, petting....
Each time 1 went to bowl they had to break free of all these hands and arms.
It was like a race to be the last one to touch the bowler, right up until they've thrown. like the hands and arms in drag me to hell expect brown and sensual
so bizarre.
why do they do this?
pic rel but brown and stinky

62460768
>>62455914
I for one love my country. And like true love I hate all those that threaten to jeopardize it. Enjoy your stay but you have to go back

62460799
fun fact: indians skin is brown because they each eat up to 4lbs of poop daily, like how flamingos turn pink from their diet of shrimp!

62460937
>>62460799
Oh I thought flamingos turned pink because they ate pussy

62461696
saaaaaaaaaaaars

62462254
>>62460799
wtf

62462262
>>62449388
All these office chairs are basically painted fabric. The paint flakes off after a while. They shouldn't be able to sell this junk in the first place.

62462271
>>62449388
>People
americans are not people.

62462287
>>62449441
Why do Indians talk so weird? It's like you can immediately tell they're jeets just from text alone

62462621
>>62462271
Seethe

62462950
>>62449388
I've seen people abuse their return program, and get their membership canceled.

62462960
>>62453201
i would cover her face with semen if you catch my drift

62463582
>>62449441
BENCHOOOOOD!!!!!!! WHY YOU REDEEM!! NO NO DO NOT CLICK ON THAT!!!! NOOOOOOOOOOO!!!!!!

62463635
>>62458606
cucks here won't do shit, they bitch and complain while patronizing Indian establishments. They will talk about the take over and then also about their favorite totally awesome pajeet they met at signhortons. The most opposition you will get is from white canadians. People will whine about the state of tim hortons while simultaneously pretending a coffee from there is a mandatory purchase they will die without

Sad, but they will have to watch canada just disintegrate with no option to fix it before they maybe realize. This whole country is full of just chicken shit low IQ cattle. Maybe 4% of canadians realize how bad this actually is and are doing the bare minimum like not hiring and giving money to the invaders, the rest are fully, entirely complicit in it. If it wasn't for how big this place was, that you can find thousands of acres with not a single person within 5km of you, I would have already left.

62464135
>>62463582
Saaars

62464324
>>62463635
Canada is just India now accept it

62464711
>>62462960
Kek

62464984
>>62449441
kek'd at the replies

62464997
>>62449441
I fucking hate Indians so much man

62465006
>>62449846
lol. Source?

62465010
>>62449441
easy (you)s

62465617
>>62465010
Cope

62465950
>>62449388
+1000 izzat

62466351
saaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaar

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 137 replies — 35.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 137 • Updated: 2026-07-10 06:47 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62458604
>>62449961
why do retards who ask how to get jobs never, EVER talk about their skills, experience (yes, you can be a NEET and still have experience even if you have never had a job), hobbies (that can be converted to jobs) and interests?
not asking about this particular OP, but about people in general...

62458671
You've got me searching for stuff from high school now. 20 years ago.
Jesus.

62458676
>>62458604
Because they don't think they have any skills or experience. And how would they evaluate that anyway? They have no frame of reference.

62458726
>>62458676
being ignorant about their own employable skills is exactly the reason why they should mention skills, experience, hobbies and interests.
they could simply say "I like X, Y and Z. do you think any of these things can help me get a job?". even gaymers can work as testers.


I was a NEET once upon a time, and I knew I liked computers, reading science and engineering news and stuff like that. that got me a job.

62458735
just end it bro

62458848
>>62449961
kind of fucked
your best bet if you have a car is to get a job working overnight at McDonalds or another 24 hour fast food place
if you aren't obviously a druggy or look like a psycho they will hire you on the spot, seriously
or just lie on your resume and get any menial wageslave job
just research some businesses that used to exist in your area, for example find restaurants that went out of business and say you worked as a dishwasher
if they ask about gaps in your resume you can say you were looking after a sick family member
make everything before the last 5 years or so a small business that you owned and operated yourself, for example lawnmowing

so your resume should look like this
graduated high school
worked for yourself as a believable small business like a lawnmower
"my business was destroyed by covid"
began looking after a sick relative for 2-3 years
worked as a dishwasher, or cleaning toilets, or some other thing for a business like a hotel that went out of business recently in your town or a nearby town. for 2 or so years until 6-12 months ago when that business closed
been searching for a job since then

problems with this method:
- if they check references you're fucked but no one does for these dead end jobs like McDonalds maintainence or dishwasher. there are services you can hire which pretend to be references for you but it shouldn't be necessary
- if you don't have a car you're just fucked, you need it to have a leg up on other applicants for these jobs. I'm pretty sure I was hired by McDonalds the moment I said I had my own vehicle and could be available to start at 2am
- if you get the job you're trapped in a hell dead end wage slave job

source: same situation as you OP but I was 29 rather than 39

62459478
>>62458735
Why, my life is better than yours.
>>62458848
I live miles from any McDonald's. I've got a convenience store, a place that used to be a bowling alley but turned into some other business I've never been in, OR a several mile trip along a freeway.

62460298
>>62449961
>>62449979
Some sort of low entry Zogbot job;
Coast Guard is easy
National Guard idk
Navy
Airforce

Avoid anything army related and you'll be alright.

t. Same boat but turned it around

62460390
op unironically save up enough money for a pressure washer and sign up for one of those indepdent contractor apps and start pressure washing boomers drive ways. save up enough to buy or lease a car then pivot into driving uber.


this is your only realistic path to making any kind of money beyond minimum wage

62460633
>>62460390
By driving for Uber? You okay man? You wanna talk about it?

62460654
>>62449961
Most strip malls have a staffing agency that would jump if anyone without a felony came in. It'll suck at the beginning but that's what you get for wasting 23 years of your life.

62460716
>>62460633
?

uber drivers crack $40/hour where I am

62460737
>>62460716
Well, that's unusual. They're getting very lucky.

62460759
>>62460737
I’d be surprised if they don’t make at least $25 an hour in most metro areas and the peripheries

where do you live if you don’t mind my asking that they are legitimately only making minimum wage?

62460789
>>62460759
>make $25 an hour
>spend double that on gas and vehicle maintenance.
How exactly is that supposed to pan out?

62460802
>>62460789
maintenance isn’t that much if you have a newer car, and I’m pretty sure driving some shit box is against uber rules anyway you need to register with a relatively new car


gas eh maybe you have a point but they’re still out so it’s obviously profitable

I highly doubt uber drivers are making minimum wage except in some super rural area where everyone had a car. And I know for a fact they make decent money where I live

62461444
>>62460802
So I need a high paying job to get a nice new car so I can ruin it working for a lower paying job?
I live in California, gas alone would fuck me sideways. If I had a nice car I'd leave. Go to Oregon or Washington.

62461781
I just realized I'm going to need to learn how to pay taxes.

62462300
>>62461781
https://www.irs.gov/individuals/free-tax-return-preparation-for-qualifying-taxpayers if you make less than 69k a year you probably qualify

62462365
trucking. self-driving trucks are a meme and still decades away, if you're a company driver you can pull down 80k clean with little overhead.

most companies will hire you on the spot if you have a CDL and a clean record

62462409
>>62459478
yeah you arre going to have to drive miles to your job
you DO have a car right op?

62462413
>>62462365
>if you have a CDL
they have pivoted to only hiring jeets as of a year or two ago

62462416
>>62460802
it's all jeets and other browns that cheat the system by having 3 drivers share the same car and uber account
you're lucky to make minimum wage after gas, tax and wear and tear

62462448
>>62462416
Actually, sharing a car makes sense. You at least split the cost of buying a car between you. And the insurance.

62463196
>>62462448
Triple the maintenance, but you have support from your other drivers

62464516
Is playing the lottery viable?

62465774
Sorry, I need the thread to stay up a little longer.

62465843
>>62449961
Similar to you OP, 40 years old, only 1 year office job experience.
Although I did learn programming in my free time. I am also taking care of me and my mother's estate which includes 3 rental properties and a sizable stock portfolio. I also taught myself ancient Greek, some Japanese, and some Russian. I read about business and finance daily.
I'm pretty sure I could be useful in an office setting, especially one finance or real estate related. Not necessarily because of my real estate or finance knowledge, but by being very comfortable using unix utilities, setting up servers, writing programs and scripts, and setting up web services. That, in addition to understanding, tax, finance, and renting.

I just don't know how to frame all these for an employer. I'm thinking it'd be easier to start an online agency.

62466261
>>62465843
You sound like you've got it made compared to me. Good luck on your agency.

62466338
>>62449961
Close your eyes.
Imagine a career.
Now make it into a resume.
There is no employment gap.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 129 replies — 35.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 129 • Updated: 2026-07-10 06:59 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

62454511
>>62426638
this but unironically

62454976
I sold at 115k so no more plans for me.

62455432
>>62454976
Ok

62455827
>>62426535
How you doing now, bobo?

62459212
>>62455827
Lmao

62459912
>>62444587
I like the idea behind their TBV, as it helps to keep all transactions on the BTC chain

62463121
>>62450912
Depends on you

62464112
>>62459912

62466089
>>62450895
ofcourze.. keep accumulating

62466111
Epstein pedo coin radioactive garbage

62466317
I'm just sticking to DCA instead of trying to catch the exact bottom. Every cycle has these pullbacks and guessing the lowest entry usually ends up costing more than just buying consistently. I'm curious how Candora's Liquidity Mirror will show how liquidity changes around key price levels once it launches.

62466320
Just get it through your head that it’s down until Trump is out bare minimum and then it entirely depends on whatever degree of a travesty the next administration is. Trump’s presidency is a bust at this point though. He’s going to fuck shit up and run insider trading scams on the market until the party is over.

62466322
I'd rather wait until BTC shows some actual strength before adding more. Catching falling knives sounds good until it doesn't. Candora's Anti-Spoof feature is expected to help distinguish more consistent liquidity from orders that disappear quickly once it launches.

62466325
Depends on what's driving the move. If it's just leverage getting flushed then I'm not too worried, but if macro conditions keep getting worse I'd expect more downside first. I'm interested in how Candora Agent will help analyze market events alongside price action once it launches.

62466346
I'm buying small but keeping plenty of cash on the side. Nobody knows where the bottom is, so position sizing matters more than making the perfect call. Candora's Slippage Protection will help reduce the impact of unexpected execution price changes once it launches.

62466355
The more everyone starts calling for a crash, the more I start looking for buying opportunities. Fear usually creates better entries than hype. I'm curious how Candora's Order Shield will briefly evaluate sudden price spikes before triggering stop orders once it launches.

item.title: Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production — 79 replies — 25.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Swift is rolling out its own blockchain right now in live production

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-07-10 07:34 UTC

OP

DTCC is going live on-chain later this year.
Yet crypto is in the shitter, why?

Early replies (first 3)

62463146
>>62463144
Explain how it's bullish for crypto?

62463270
>>62463146
Both Swift and DTCC are going to be using a variety of public chains.

62463273
So SWIFT has their own singular chain. I thought it'd be that each bank has their own chain, connected to each other by CCIP. If it's all done on a single chain then it obviates the need for a lot of CCIP transactions, right?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62464488
>>62463146
Chain link is what powers it. Every transaction will have fees paid for in chain link and people will have to stake chain link so that the data nodes are secure. Demand goes up chain link goes $10k each. Study more

62464845
>>62463752
/thread

its funny how the link cult can't understand how the rest of the investment world moved on from the space and woke up years ago about how all of the announcements dont mean anything for altcoins


OP sounds like a boomer complaining no one wants to work anymore

62464853
>>62464488
>duuuuuuuude just research moar buzzzzzwordssss!

yeah hows that going for ya kid

62465008
>>62463808
kek its always funny reading all the mind broken crippletards and the fudkeks here. like
>NONONO YOU CANT MEAN THAT IT IS ALL FAKE YOU MUST WORK WITH XRP OTHERWISE WHAT HAVE I WASTED ALL THESE YEARS FOR

62465020
>>62464853
how's this adem?
https://x.com/The_DTCC/status/2075280707203592232

be honest now, how much did you lose in Wormhole, LayerZero and LinkSol?
imagine sinking your money in literal SCAMS just to stick it to le EBIL linkies and walking away even poorer LMAO

62465065
>>62465020
I hold 1,500 since im a poorfag. Been on this board since 2022 and have seen so many posts about dates and how chainlink will finally rise in price like when they claimed CCIP would make it rise, november of last year because that guy from swift says that they are done piloting, sergey going to the fucking white house and now the clarity act. I dont believe anything until it actually happens, until then ill just keep buying when i can.

62465075
>>62463686
/Biz/ would be fun for a few days

62465077
>>62463552
Now that's what I'm talking about

62465091
>>62463144
>Agentic commerce

Either these buzzwords die or I become very wealthy, winwin

62465124
>>62463144
TOKEN NOT NEEDED

62465212
>>62463146
It’s bullish for chainlink, which is all that matters

62465220
>>62465212
nothing is bullish for chainlink sweetie
it is an eternal dump coin of dumpage

62465269
tick tock fuddies

62465459
>>62463144
>>62463348
everything to do with chainlink

62465696
>>62463428
It isn't though.

62465711
>>62463144
>kek, thanks for the free case study and stress-testing on blockchain networks, suckers!

62465827
Testimg

62465841
>>62463144
>Swift is rolling out its own blockchain
it isn't
a private chain is worthless/ meaningless

62465848
>>62463146
mood! It was supposed to be XRP but they just cucked ceypto and made their own token.

62465917
>>62463144

ICP and DOT

https://youtu.be/W2A53FoHnjs

62466023
>>62465065
I held 2500+ at one point and also went through a belief phase. I remember religiously playing the link rap song, genuinely catchy. Eric Schmidt and sergey fireside was the peak of my hopium and I remember saying to myself this was it, this had to have been it, I mean the CEO of Google in its heyday giving props to the dev of my bags, it felt better than 2016 Trump winning. Every link marine on /biz/ was hyping each other up. Holy fuck Eric Schmidt just told sergey he was a genius and this was the next industrial revolution. Smart contract assassins holy FUCK!

Then the dopamine faded and the price chugged on as usual and more and more breadcrumbs started spawning with more and more dates. Ended up selling all at around $18 thinking I'd slurp at single digits and finally make it but have yet to buy back, I don't think I have it in me to baghold for more dates desu

62466028
>>62465841
What part of
>not needed
is not getting through to you?

62466034
>>62463670
>No, but 4=4

62466154
Pornlayer peelers shedding at an extreme rate

62466163
i need more linkies

62466164
>>62465917
>ICP
I bet you do, buddy.

62466376
>>62465841
>>62465696
>>62465917
The Swift ledger is built specifically to be connected to many chains, private and public.
The future is multichain, haven't you heard?

You L1 maxis really need to wipe the shit from your eyeballs already.

62466383
>>62466028
what the fuck are you talking about
>>62466376
>built specifically to be connected to many chains, private and public
you only need 1 public chain for ordering, that's it
and since ordering is done there, the liquidity is also there
a private chain is an oxymoron because you don't need to order anything,you're not going to sandwich attack/double spend your own fucking transaction
this is apparently what xrpiglets didn't understand yet concerning this "tech" and are hopelessly illiterate on

62466401
>>62466383
>you only need 1 public chain

L1 maxi detected.
The future is multichain, how are you not wise to this yet holy shit.

62466434
it's all so tiresome
https://x.com/ChrisBarrett/status/1972765646976123278
